text: The long time theatre director from my high school is retiring at the end of the school year quietly. However, a few of us alumni are trying to figure out how to celebrate him so that he gets a proper sendoff (most of us leaning towards Summer Shakespeare in July). What a gift he gave us with his guidance. And the next person who will be in charge has learned from him over the years (plus I've known her since she was born). It's beautiful.

Due to some new opportunities and the need to reprioritize with the season (all good things, thankfully!) my chapter in the hedgie house is coming to a close for now. I’ve enjoyed writing alongside you for the past couple of years so much! Wishing you all the best as you continue working on your stories. :yellow_heart: I’ll still be following along over on Instagram and can’t wait to see updates on your writing journeys!

Think of it like a working lunch with Hedgie sisters!

*1:00 PM        HEADS DOWN*

*4:00 PM        HEADS DOWN*

*7:00 PM        SOCIAL MEDIA SUNDAY OPEN TABLE |* Struggling to keep up with your author Instagram page? Not sure where to start? Join your hedgie sisters to brainstorm a strategy that works for you. Let’s turn your author marketing from a stressor into a cup filler!

*8:30 PM        POMODORO PRICKLE OPEN TABLE |* A 90-minute Prickle with three 25 min. blocks of uninterrupted writing with a five-minute break. The first 5 minutes consist of welcoming and dropping in the chat what you are working on, maybe leading a stretch or a quick meditation followed by the first 25-minute session, 5-minute mediation/stretch, second 25 min, and then the same for the last one. You do NOT have to commit to the full 90 minutes. Hopping on/off when you're able is totally okay!

*10:00 PM      PLOT OR PLAN PRICKLE with <@U0351JBGQHZ> |* Let's shoo those Sunday Night Scaries away together and take the time to plan! Take the hour to plot your story or to plan and prep for the week ahead!

*7:00 AM         HEADS DOWN*

*10:00 AM      OPEN TABLE*

*11:00 AM      MARKETING POWER HOUR / #AUTHORLIFE HEADS DOWN |* Join us in making progress on our #authorlife marketing stuff. We'll start with a check-in and then use the hour to get work done on your marketing in a social, supportive way. Feel free to unmute throughout as questions arise. Think of it like a working lunch with Hedgie sisters!

*1:00 PM        HEADS DOWN*

*4:00 PM        HEADS DOWN*

*7:00 PM        SOCIAL MEDIA SUNDAY OPEN TABLE |* Struggling to keep up with your author Instagram page? Not sure where to start? Join your hedgie sisters to brainstorm a strategy that works for you. Bounce ideas for your pillars around and share tips and tricks to save you time and reduce stress—especially with the tasks you enjoy the least. Let’s turn your author marketing from a stressor into a cup filler!

*8:30 PM        POMODORO PRICKLE OPEN TABLE |* A 90-minute Prickle with three 25 min. blocks of uninterrupted writing with a five-minute break. The first 5 minutes consist of welcoming and dropping in the chat what you are working on, maybe leading a stretch or a quick meditation followed by the first 25-minute session, 5-minute mediation/stretch, second 25 min, and then the same for the last one. You do NOT have to commit to the full 90 minutes. Hopping on/off when you're able is totally okay!

*10:00 PM      PLOT OR PLAN PRICKLE with <@U0351JBGQHZ> |* Let's shoo those Sunday Night Scaries away together and take the time to plan! Take the hour to plot your story or to plan and prep for the week ahead!

*11:30 PM       MIDNIGHT CREW*

*6:00 AM        HEADS DOWN with <@U09SMP5QAN8>* 

*7:00 AM        <@U08A1A5HCMU>* 

*10:00 AM      OPEN TABLE*

*11:00 AM      MARKETING POWER HOUR / #AUTHORLIFE HEADS DOWN |* Join us in making progress on our #authorlife marketing stuff. We'll start with a check-in and then use the hour to get work done on your marketing in a social, supportive way. Feel free to unmute throughout as questions arise. Think of it like a working lunch with Hedgie sisters!

*1:00 PM        HEADS DOWN*

*4:00 PM        HEADS DOWN*

*7:00 PM        SOCIAL MEDIA SUNDAY OPEN TABLE |* Struggling to keep up with your author Instagram page? Not sure where to start? Join your hedgie sisters to brainstorm a strategy that works for you. Bounce ideas for your pillars around and share tips and tricks to save you time and reduce stress—especially with the tasks you enjoy the least. Let’s turn your author marketing from a stressor into a cup filler!

*8:30 PM        POMODORO PRICKLE OPEN TABLE |* A 90-minute Prickle with three 25 min. blocks of uninterrupted writing with a five-minute break. The first 5 minutes consist of welcoming and dropping in the chat what you are working on, maybe leading a stretch or a quick meditation followed by the first 25-minute session, 5-minute mediation/stretch, second 25 min, and then the same for the last one. You do NOT have to commit to the full 90 minutes. Hopping on/off when you're able is totally okay!

*10:00 PM      PLOT OR PLAN PRICKLE with <@U0351JBGQHZ> |* Let's shoo those Sunday Night Scaries away together and take the time to plan! Take the hour to plot your story or to plan and prep for the week ahead!

*11:30 PM       <@U03G88335AP>*

{"text":"*Random Helpful Tip (that I often give directly to people, but figured I'd include it here):* If you have a website, you can eliminate the need for a secondary "Links" site (such as Linktree) by simply creating your own on your website. You can even hide it from the normal navigation bar, so you only access it from where you've intentionally linked it (such as your IG). Also, if you have a newsletter, you can include a signup below the links! Makes signing up easier for anyone wanting to. This also doubles as directing traffic to your website, which makes search engines (such as Google) recognize it as more popular and it is then likely to be shown higher in search results.

*Important Note:* Part of why this is so helpful is that most social media platforms notoriously suppress any use of the word "link" - including in "linktree". So, posts that include mention of linktree or statements like "link in BIO" are usually suppressed in the algorithms. (That's why people often use a chainlink emote instead of the word!) This is because the companies that own them (such as Meta) don't WANT you to direct people to other websites - they want users to stay on THEIR website. This has been shown through a lot of users doing A / B testing over the years; results typically showed that posts that included words like "link" would get almost no traction (if any at all) and the exact same posts without the word "link" would reach users/followers perfectly fine.

Included images to show my link of my IG and my link page on my website. ALSO, note, I should probably change the URL to NOT include the word "links" in it. I just haven't got around to it, yet. text:
The lesson I'm learning right now is that starting is hard, but the real power lies in finishing what you start, and to stay tenacious in that journey to the finish line -- especially in the final furlong.
I'm beginning to understand that the last push to the finish line is excruciating, but precisely where you discover what you're made of.
Chin up, shoulders back, vision narrowed. Persist, complete, celebrate.

text:
Hey hedgies! Since I posted waaayyyy back in February about the old lady cat's health and the scame in hedgie huddle, 12-ish weeks have passed and our chaotic season kept going. Car stuff (started with Ant's car and then mine), owing on taxes, elongated lower back pain (me), the old lady cat's health isn't resolved but maybe we have a next step.
Life is a wild, chaotic ride and we're realizing we're not in charge of each detail or the future as much as we'd like to be. On top of that, I have struggled with my job change (went from part-time daycare teacher/write life/home life to full time front office secretary) and realized about 2-3 months in that I couldn't stick this out for a solid year. I realized that I missed working with kids way more than I thought I would. And, with Ant's blessing, I started looking for daycare/child-related jobs again, possibly even considering teaching.
And well, last friday morning, I interviewed at a daycare that's bigger than the one I worked at (more toddlers and more/younger babies), buuuut before my interview I got to help toddlers plant strawberries and say hi to goats AND during my interview I got to hold a baby! Ant and I were worried about part-time job status (because life and bills and wanting to be homeowners), but the daycare supervisors seemed excited that my availability was fulltime, and they offered me the job Monday (gosh, was that only yesterday?). I accepted! I'm so relieved to work with kids again. It'll still be full time, but possibly I'll get off work on time and my drive to and from the daycare will be prettier. I'll go back to school to obtain some early childhood education units, which is wild to think that I'll be going back to school! (It's also coming at a time when Ant's job is going in a direction he's not comfortable with.)
I still need to turn in my resignation and tell my co-workers (which I'm dreading, but I know I need to do it.) All this to say that life is chaotic and wild and this is a plot twist neither Ant or I expected.
So here's me just encouraging you all to lean into the little moments throughout the day if life feels hard or dark or like you're in a really long tunnel and can see just a few feet in front of you. If you ever need anything, I'm just a message away:yellow_heart:

text:
Hi lovelies!

Here's your synthesized feedback from the Winter 2026 Virtual Retreat. It's not necessary for you to read, but I like sharing it for transparency and accountability :blush: .

Thank you for allowing me to continue curating these meaningful experiences for - and with - you. I'm so grateful you're continuing to find them worth your time.

A standout testimonial from this last one made me laugh: "This was my first retreat and I'm pretty pissed with myself for not doing one sooner. Just saying."

Moving forward, I'll be thinking of a mindset-related topic that also allows us to dig into craft -- and dreaming up a kit I can mail you again!

Thanks again for your feedback; it literally builds whatever comes next. :heart:

text:
*Wednesday’s Accountability: Here's your nothing-new/catch up/catch your breath day!*

Look at your areas of responsibility (Writing, Family, Marriage, etc.).. your values.. and see what's aligned for you to "catch up" on. This might not be writing at all, but just in case it is! What would fill your cup to catch up on? A Daily Coaching video? A Daily Accountability question from a week ago? FINALLY listen to THAT webinar you've been meaning to get in your brain? Or maybe you finally wrote that social media post or newsletter you've been putting off? Tell us what you "caught up" on today!

*Tuesday’s Accountability:* In preparation of our FIRST Open Mic Prickle (happening later today at NOON EST and again on Thursday at 8pm EST) we want to know... are you usually the one walking up to the mic first? Sitting in the front row waiting for someone to hand it to you? Sitting in the third row happy to blend in and listen? Or in the fifth, working up the courage to share? How are you wanting to show up today? How can we support you?

If you're wanting to share, message <@U06FA58C4B1> to see if there's room! :microphone:

*Monday’s Accountability:*

1. Schedule ONE Prickle that you'll attend this week! Share it for accountability. Then, share your just-manageable writing/Quill & Cup goals this week. Look back at your response on Friday to see if you need to revise your goal in anyway to make it both just-challenging enough and realistic to accomplish!`

2. What’s a cup-filling activity you’ll include in your week? When?

text:
*Tuesday’s Accountability*: In the Quill & Cup Self-Editing Academy (doors open SO SOON!!), Ania has found the prompt “What would your main character say about ___ to a stranger?” to be incredibly potent in revealing backstory. Here’s a chance to practice this, too!

1. *Choose a character pairing*: your main character and a side character, for example, or two main characters.
2. *Then, ask yourself the question, “What would your MC say to a stranger about ___?”*
3. *Leave the answer in the comments!* 

Drop a :fire:after your answer if participating in this prompt helped elucidate some new information about your characters for you!

text: I just ran into a colleague who told me she found my Instagram account. Nobody at work except my boss knows that I'm a writer. I tend to keep my writing and professional lives veeeery separate (and also not as separate as I could because my Instagram account is publicly visible under my name, so this was bound to happen eventually, and now I need to figure out my comfort level with having my name associated with that account). Anyways, this colleague was very kind about it and I did not throw up or pass out talking to her about my writing. I'm going to go hyperventilate for a bit, but I'm also going to consider making it through that conversation a win. I don't talk about my writing "in the wild" at all ever and I probably need to make more of an effort in order to get more comfortable doing it.

text:
I'm proud of myself for advocating for my needs _despite_ being denied and pushed back against! 

Don’t necessarily know if I have a case to argue, but I'm sure gonna try! (Am I just going crazy? Possibly…)

My airline I’m flying next month denied Tripp - a service dog in training - because their policy only accommodates service animals in training who are affiliated with training organizations they're partnered with. But that policy isn't mentioned ANYWHERE on their website. So I filed a complaint:persevere: Extremely nervous because: yes, the airline has the right to deny Tripp. My argument is they're using a policy I never read.

text:
Not a writing update but a health one: For the first time since I was 11, I have been able to tolerate sunlight without frying like a freaking vampire after 5 minutes of exposure wearing SPF 30-50.
3 days in a row I've been sitting outside without SPF for an hour at a time, and only had MINIMAL allergic reactions on the smallest patch of skin that disappeared very quickly.
Guys, if you know how debilitating it is to be unable to tolerate sunlight to such a level, I can't stress how liberating this feels to me.
The treatment I've been on since January works miracles :sunglasses: :sunny:

text: :exploding_head:I had the epiphany of all epiphanies today!! I figured out how to fix the Opening Chapter of my fantasy book. It just clicked on what I was missing, and now it has that focus it's been begging to have. A true focus on Sarasin's old life and actual disruption, focusing on the themes I'll be tackling in the story, and introduce key world building in a way that's not a lore dump, as well as a couple of key characters that help drive Sarasin forward in her journey. I'm so hyped to go in and rework it and I feel so great about it omggggg :conga_parrot:

text:
HUGE breakthrough on Ravens this morning. Like I have spent the last month banging my head against the wall on how to tie up the last 5 chapters of this book, and this morning I FINALLY figured it out. I also wrote at 6 a.m. for the first time in foreeeever which felt amazing, and now that I've done the "figuring out" in my notebook I'm hoping I can get back onto some 6am prickles to draft those last five chapters out. After a very long, very sleepy winter I feel like my morning writer brain is FINALLY kicking back into gear and I am so, so excited to have the energy to get to the end of this draft!

Also, I was so in flow that Olivia woke HERSELF up and came into my room to tell me we were late for school. So let's also hype my kid for having better track of time than me :sweat_smile:

Hyping up <@U01NYR15VE0> because WOW! After her educational prickle last night on who raised your protagonist and the invisible wound cycle, I was able to outline my new project and everything clicked. I have two huge takeaways that I know will impact every story I write from here forward. THANK YOU!

text: I wrote The End on my book (again) last night. :partying_face: This was the 7th or 8th revision of the book I dipped into the query trenches summer. In the fall, I decided to make some changes based on agent and professional feedback. The changes I expected to take 2ish months ended up triggering a pretty major overhaul. The characters, settings, and heart of the story stayed the same---and I was able to recycle/repurpose many of the scenes. But the premise and plot changed a TON... for what feels like the better. :smiling_face_with_3_hearts: Unfortunately, I'm like 35K over my target wordcount, so I have a lot of cutting to do. But I'm delighted to dig in!

text:
At about the halfway mark of our current challenge QC20, I changed my goal to hit 15K instead of the full 20K because I was _so_ worried I wouldn't make it. But I knew that 15K would be doable. My resting days were still spend writing, but without pressure and mostly by hand. 

With 3 days left, I’m proud to say that I’m only 336 words away from 20K:blob_excited: Don't ask me what happened! The answer is: I don't know:joy: It could be a handful of different things. But I'm SOOOOOOO happy with my progress and my writing streak (40 days today!) and have renewed hope of finishing it by my self-imposed deadline of May 31st. I am giving myself a little leeway until June 8th because I start up my college courses again on June 9th:hedgehog-joy:

(Also, sorry not sorry for hyping myself up a lot recently!)

text:
I'm just going over my tax papers and under profession I am seeing the word: AUTHOR. :sob::sob::sob: 

Past me was so discouraged by our past tax person that I never looked into claiming it again until she retired last year. This time…no questions asked. I'm so glad I tried again! :face_holding_back_tears:

CBOI is almost at the 40K mark (~35K)! I’ve added a total of just over 1700 words between typing and handwriting. Chapter 13 is wrapping up:partying_face:

This is gonna be a thick book!! I currently don't know how many chapters - that's a this weekend type of goal - but I don't think I need to worry about hitting my overall WC goal of 120K:joy:

text: I like tracking things, specifically writing things. So I made a megatracker for all my projects, in this case I tracked the work hours to see where I was at while recovering from Covid, and the results surprised me. As I got better, I not only managed to write/edit/whatever more consistently, but I had far more capacity and drive to actually do it. I'm enjoying it far more than I have in years. I haven't worked this much in maybe 12 years since my health said eff you and sayonara. The treatments I've done/am doing are definitely working miracles.

text:
A hype and a big thank you to <@U03JN2L76NB> for taking an hour and a half out of her evening to chat with me. 

I'm so encouraged to dream big because of you, sister:blob_aww::rainbow_sparkles:

So... I've been sitting on this for a few weeks but I was so nervous in the beginning I couldn't even think about it or talk about it. March 1st I queried my DREAM agent. Eleven days later she requested a full. On query tracker, you can see how many queries an agent gets, so I checked it out today just to see. In the past 3 months she's received about 607 queries. Out of all those, she's only requested 3 full manuscripts and mine is one of them. :face_holding_back_tears: I'm not a math genius but that's like 1 out of every 200 or 0.5% and I'm trying to be proud of myself in this moment. I've been so worried sick about her possibly saying no that I forgot to celebrate where I'm at right now and I just wanted to share it with people who get it. :crossed_fingers::heartpulse::smiling_face_with_3_hearts:

A little reminder for all of us to stop bracing and celebrate this moment while we’re in it.
I FILED MY TAXES!!! :tada::tada::tada::tada::tada:

I just LOVE squeezing Hedgies in their home cities!! Thank you for having breakfast with us in Milwaukee today, <@U03VAUYKJ6Q> and Jeff! 

If you’d ever be down for the Rays to come visit you randomly as we’re passing through, drop your town and state in the thread below! I'll see it once and I tend to remember!

My daughter and I took a road trip to Saratoga NY. I. Love. It. Here. As I sit in the hotel overlooking downtown, I can't help but wonder what life must be like living here. Beautiful day. Great company. Fun location. I am so grateful to have this opportunity with her.

text: Olivia LOVES making things. (She's like her dad in that she loves working with her hands.) Nick is the dedicated craft parent, because in Olivia's words, "Dad has all the cool tools." (He used to be a carpenter so he does have cool tools.) Tonight they drilled perfect holes in a cardboard box for an arcade game she wanted to build (I think it's her version of whack-a-mole). It has been a VERY long week for me. Watching them tonight filled my cup in a way I don't have words for. Also, this evening's Olivia quote rocks: "The more games you play, the funner your life's gonna be!"

text:
My cup is over flowing with joy from my therapy session today. Tripp hasn't come with me in a month or so because she was whining in excitement at everyone she saw last time. We've been working on it and today it paid off!!!

Only a couple of times did she get a little excited about people - one being a kid so it made sense. But she didn't make any vocal sounds (and she's part husky) and mostly stayed in her lay position at my feet while we waited. While in my therapists office, she sniffed a little bit around - which I was okay with since she is still training - before laying next to me on the couch (my therapist is chill with that). 

Even my therapist made a couple comments about her behavior and training since last time she attended with me:blush: It probably helped that we walked to and from the office (it's a block from where I live) but she's a mix with a handful of high energy breeds so it's a major win!

text:
The way my inner parts are taking shape and multiplying by the dozen is one of the most cup-filling (and exhausting, but in the best way :rolling_on_the_floor_laughing:) experiences I’ve ever had. 

Just wrote the introduction scene for baby Nutmeg—a saw-whet/little owl hybrid, the part who pre-emptively translates in order to be well-received—and Willow the blossom bat—who masks to be accepted and stay safe in social context—inserted herself into the scene and they ended up crawling into Elin’s hoodie pocket together and it's the cutest frigging thing ever and it is now my life’s purpose to draw an accompanying illustration to go with the Substack post this will become :face_holding_back_tears:

I just submitted a document to help the students at my former high school for their spring play. They're doing Harry Potter and the Cursed Child: School Edition and I asked my former director if he would like me to do a write up to help the cast and crew since they weren't born when the series began. Just doing all this research and writing for them made my heart so full because I love supporting the kids in that program any way I can. My high school they're department was truly life saving for me so giving back to them any way I can just brings me so much joy. 

The document is also 18 pages and over 3,000 words.

Met up with <@U057A5E8N7J> today for a much needed hedgie hang out! We went to a coffee shop for an in-person writing group, then lunch and a quick peruse of a book shop before taking off :smiling_face_with_3_hearts:.

text:
So, I wrote a children's chapter book recently. About 5k words? The difficult part of children's books (ages 6-9 for this one) is getting the language within the right range that it challenges them a little bit but are still words they can understand. And having a character they can relate to with a lesson or moral that makes sense. So, the moral of my story was "friendship is the real magic" after the forest's magic disappeared. This was for a class and I got 9.5/10! Its worth 25% of my final grade and I am SO HYPED about this! My prof left an audio note and said how I could improve the ending and opening a bit, but that my language and moral were perfect for the age group, and the characters (a talking squirrel named Pip and a young witch named Mira who has lost her magic) are relatable and interesting to kids. She said she'd love to see me move forward with this and seek publishing it someday. :heart:
EDIT: For context, my prof is a published children's author.
EDIT 2: Also, this was the first "The End" on a book I've ever written. Before, I had only done short stories.

text: I wanted to post this because I shared my _Newhall_ pitch in the pitch prickle today for the first time.:face_holding_back_tears: A lot of you know it was a real place, and 19th-century hotels usually had stationery and envelopes for guests. Newhall was only around from 1857 - 1883, and there isn't much remaining from it. I only saw one of these before, and I'm not kidding that I checked eBay weekly for over two years and finally got this Newhall envelope postmarked 1878. The title of my novel is just going to be _Newhall_ and I am going to request if trad pub'd/do myself if indie, that the book title is created with this original font. :face_holding_back_tears: It's a love song to the real thing and Milwaukee history. :purple_heart:

text:
I tend to stick to the same 3 coffee shops in my neighborhood because I'm a creature of habit and they're close by. But today they were literally ALL full so I had to go a little further afield to find a place to write (husband and kid are home today so focus time in our house is nonexistent :sweat_smile:). Anyways I found this tiiiiny hole-in-the-wall place about a 10 min drive from my house that I had no idea existed. It's run by an Iranian couple who set up a salon/coffee shop duo business. The husband was working the cafe today and he was so, so lovely and told me to stick around as long as I wanted. And also I was 100% convinced I died and went to heaven when I had a bite of the apple tart ... 

I think I found a cute new writing haunt and I'm so excited. Sitting in quiet coffee shops is basically my favourite thing to do, especially when they're as cute and the food as delicious as this one :blob_excited:

A bookstore just ghosted me, but I got this lovely email today!! Laughing because author life can change so rapidly. One minute you've been ghosted by the bookstore that approached _you._ The next, another shop is like, "This sounds good, but didn't you also have a witchy series?" Um YES, in fact I do:star-struck:

text:
I am trying to practice more patience this year (more patience, less perfectionism—and I'm realizing that the less perfectionist I am, the better things turn out and the less patience I seem to need to have?? Anyway…) one of the things I told myself I'd have patience with is sourdough. 

I've killed sooo many starters.

I've NEVER gotten bubbles like this! 

I think… I *think* I cracked the code! :heart_eyes::heart_eyes: I can't wait to bake bread! And then snack on said bread while writing:heart_eyes::heart_eyes::heart_eyes::heart_eyes:

text:
I took tomorrow off and literally the only person who knows about it is my boss. Not my husband (I forgot to tell him :sweat_smile: ). Not anybody else in my family (when they know I have a day off they suddenly show up). My team will find out AFTER their day begins tomorrow so my boss can field their questions for me (they do this thing where all 8 of them bombard me for an hour before I leave if they know I will be away :sweat_smile:). My stealth mode day off will include naps, reading and SO. MUCH. WRITING. I haven't had solid, uninterrupted time with my Ravens in almost two weeks and I am DYING.

Now to pick the perfect cafe to do that in. :raised_hands:
Unfortunately, getting outside and going on a run actually did the trick and fixed my mood. So… here's your reminder to go grab some sunshine if you can.

text:
Of course, I suck at taking pictures of events I go to. BUUUUUT I went to a Midnight Release party for GAME ON - the third _Into Darkness_ novel - last night.
Did I wake up too exhausted to go to work? Yup!
Was it worth it? Totally!
A handful of local authors were there as well as a lot of readers. It was more fun than my introverted self would've anticipated!

text:
Packing for the UK while listening to RAYE's absolutely sublime (and very aptly named) new album, THIS MUSIC MAY CONTAIN HOPE.
The whole album is a storytelling masterpiece (check out _Life Boat._ and affirm "I'm not giving up yet" out loud with it, and _Click Clack Symphony._ which features the actual Hans Zimmer!).

But _I Will Overcome._ was the one that made me sob tears of musical humanity. :musical_note:

<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=25c0gefKKDg>

I hadn't realized I needed to slow down like this.

text:
I really do LOVE writing at this bookstore! A young girl snagged a copy of _Shapeshifters_ today and Bailey told her I was upstairs. She came up and asked me to personalize her book.

Is this was it feels like to make it as an author!? Somebody pinch me!! I have to be dreaming!

Writing is slow today, but at least I made a young girl happy by personalizing her book.

text: I think the best part of this event was the “just us” nature of it all. I wasn't stressed. I DID feel vulnerable in the invite to the other mamas — very much “it's my birthday and I hope people come” nerves. But people showed up and it was wonderful talking to women who also love story. :face_holding_back_tears:
